head.hi: Memorandum by the Secretary of State

div.p:
The Chinese Minister called and referred to the fact that Italy had proposed to raise the rank of Minister to China to that of Ambassador, and inquired whether this Government would be likewise disposed. He stated that Secretary KelloggFrank B. Kellogg, Secretary of State, 1925–29. on a former occasion had sounded
out other governmentsSee Foreign Relations, 1928, vol. ii, pp. 201 ff. to ascertain whether they would be disposed to raise this rank to that of Ambassador, but that for some reason the matter failed. He indicated genuine interest in favorable action.

I replied that I was surprised to learn about the Italian movement, which I only knew about through the public print.Notations on the original documents do not indicate that the previous correspondence on this subject had been routed to the Secretary’s office. On outgoing telegrams the Secretary’s name was initialed by the Under Secretary of State, William Phillips. I added that I would give attention to his request.
